item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We can try again next time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: guess this didn't work out (again), as I learned 2 minutes ago

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The images that go with this are beautiful

item.title: Re: Licensing Information
item.content:encoded: Because of this, the third image has been removed.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: This definitely helps Bernie win.

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: Heyy, yeah, I was gone. But I'm back now! Workin on more stuff too!

item.title: Re: +200
item.content:encoded: My god, is that a wild Fantem? I really like your Pitch Haven works, but thought you were gone. Great to see you're still around.

This is how Bernie can win

Seriously though, the line about a quarter appearing in someone's lungs made me chortle.

item.title: Licensing Information
item.content:encoded:
"original.jpg" is from Wikimedia Commons; "File:Bernie Sanders (21581179719 571bb5a7ab c).jpg", taken by Phil Roeder, CC BY 2.0.

"55.png" is consisted of 3 different images from Wikimedia Commons.

  • Bernie: "File:Bernie Sanders (21581179719 571bb5a7ab c).jpg", taken by Phil Roeder, CC BY 2.0.
  • Potted plant: "File:Sedum_rubrotinctum.jpg", taken by JJ Harrison, CC BY-SA 2.5.
  • Palm trees: "File:Glare_of_the_sun_(Unsplash).jpg", taken by Clau Canabes, CC0.

"222.png" is, seemingly, a screenshot of Japanese television drama "Iguana Girl". I really don't think this image can be used under CC.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lord. Captures the ineffectual keyboard warriors I interact with on a daily basis, if they had amateur access to and understanding of real magic. Add a debate about whether the effect is anti-semitic on purpose or by accident and you've got the culture of a centrist/politically mixed American group chat down. Obviously it's going to age, but his 2020 run for president boosts that longevity, and none of the elements of the article scream "THIS WAS WRITTEN FOUR YEARS AGO" at me like many internet-aided skips do. Except maybe the vaporwave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: brilliant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't have the main problem others have with this skip. I like the earnest naïvete of the creator balanced with the jaded shitposting of GAW. I also like them interacting with modern political events, and the detail of this skip only taking affect when the meme is interacted with in a way that would make it more attractive to The All Powerful Algorithm.

I have to -1 though. As much as I want to like it, you wrote a piece where pocket change is anomalously thrown at the most prominent Jewish politician in America. Worse, he's exploiting the hopes of his naive supporters and cynically using the money for personal gain. I'm going to give you the benefit of the doubt and assume you're not aware of this, but throwing coins at Jews is a pretty common form of hate crime. If we're going to write about political figures, I do think it's our responsibility to use symbols and tropes well and responsibly. And this wasn't it chief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
since bernie is coming back into question i believe, i have been revisiting this.

It's really funny how I ended up first off hating it, then being ok with it, and then loving it lol. It's really good commentary. big fav!

item.title: legal concerns
item.content:encoded: When checking the source of the first photo it is worth mentioning that it is unclear if this picture is copyright protected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've read every comment in this section and really can't understand all the vitriol. Sure, most GAW skips revolve around someone incredibly powerful doing something incredibly stupid. But if you dislike that, then you should also dislike everything Prometheus Lab has ever did; all their skips deal with them using miracle like technology for stupid or downright ridiculous stuff and they do it with a straight face and even a sense of gravitas1. At least GAW skips understand their own silly nature and play the "An almost omnipotent secret serious grey dark ominous supra-national organization has to deal with the wacky hijinks of a bunch of forum trolls" to perfection.

Having said that, we are all entitled to our own opinions: I vocally reject the use of Prometheus Lab in every skip I have ever read that involved them. It's just that we should be honest with ourselves about the actual reasons for our distaste.

Now, about this skip. I think it has aged acceptably, it's now a picture of a particular moment in time in the ever-changing realm of internet culture and I like it a lot. Upvoted

Footnotes
1. I hate this GOI exactly for this reason

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually why I upvoted. There's a very small, very slight meta aspect to the skip in the "this might be an instance of SCP-2433-1 and by upvoting it you're doing the anomaly" thing, and that amuses me to the point that it pushes it out of novote territory for me. also i want to see the world burn

Though I do have to echo everybody else's complaints here. This SCP just sets loads of bad precedents for future skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1427 is a really good example of how the perception of art can change over time with evolving social and cultural trends. The back and forth CWW/Ophite had on that was pretty interesting.

I suppose I see your point, and the core premise of slacktivism definitely reflects the era well. But the specific IRC dialogue and memes themselves are going to be very confusing if the site is still around in, say, 2030. If the SCP wiki had been around in 1998 and there was an SCP about Bill Clinton's impeachment, it would probably still be readable, but the tone would very much be a product of its time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Actually, as much as I don't like this SCP, I will actually give it one thing; it's aged fine enough in my opinion. Bernie didn't win, but the SCP is really about someone wanting to contribute to political activism in a really sloppy, uninformed manner, which reflects a certain type of slacktivism that's unique to this era of politics well enough.

And even if this didn't age, that's fine. Plenty of SCPs on this site haven't aged well (SCP-002 and SCP-1427, respectively) and can still work regardless (like how 002 still works and how 1427 hasn't).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1. Pretty much what Kalinin said - GAW isn't something I'm inherently opposed to if the execution is done well enough (I liked the Stephen King one), but the tone and theme of articles like these doesn't appeal to me for the same reason I don't really like the quips in Marvel movies.

And, commenting in February 2018, this SCP has not aged very well.

item.title: Re: Oh jesus christ, this is an actual SCP article?
item.content:encoded: I think it's fine not to draw more attention to it if it's already been staff-posted on.

item.title: Re: Oh jesus christ, this is an actual SCP article?
item.content:encoded:
It's….an SCP designed to help Bernie win the election with….quarters?

Yes.

I thought the SCP Foundation was supposed to be scary. Not be silly like this.

I'm not exactly sure where you thought this, considering the wiki's second highest article is rock that makes you procrastinate, the ninth being a toaster that makes you think of yourself as a toaster, and its thirteenth being a racist alien. An article can be anything, as long as its well written.

Also this article screams self insert.

…where? The character that created the anomaly is a well intentioned idiot, and I'm not sure most people would want their insert to be that. But maybe that's just me.

item.title: Re: Oh jesus christ, this is an actual SCP article?
item.content:encoded:
On an unofficial note, there's no rule that the Foundation has to always be horror.

We have Joke SCPs, funny tales, Sci-Fi, etc. Not everything is horror.

item.title: Staffpost - Open
item.content:encoded:
Man of Memes, your feedback here is overly rude, and Certain Does not Help the Author, and as such is against our Criticism Policy.

While feedback as simple as "I don't like it" is perfectly fine, as is subjective opinion, shots at the author are uncalled for.

item.title: Oh jesus christ, this is an actual SCP article?
item.content:encoded:
It's….an SCP designed to help Bernie win the election with….quarters? I thought the SCP Foundation was supposed to be scary. Not be silly like this. Also this article screams self insert.

(-1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have changed my opinion on this ever since I thought more about it's backstory. Yeah, I turned to the positive side of the argument now.

1+ much better then I used to judge it on

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As much as everyone seems to venomously hate this GoI, I quite like it. It's a nice bit of levity on the website.

And a very immature part of me is upset that there's no Meme Magic SCP yet.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well that's the point, everything they do is topical, and topical things naturally become dated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gonna be honest, this is the best example I know of with regard to the old Foundation saying: There is no bad idea that cannot be saved through quality execution.

This was a risky idea for a first skip, but I'd say it paid off nicely. It's certainly original. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's strange: I think GAW is a terrible GOI, but I like this SCP. The poor guy just wanted to help Bernie :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is rather amusing.
Edit:
This SCP is basically a fundraiser for Bernie that steals quarters and and teleports them near him. Dollars might be more noticeable and pennies wouldn't add up to very much. Adding the money to his account might be complicated because it could potentially involve more factors than teleporting quarters. One would need to involve computers, banks; then there is the question as to who's name would be written on the transfer documents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a big supporter of variety, and I mostly like the idea of Gamers Against Weed - intentionally jokey articles played straight - but they're going to be much more heavily built around a narrative than most articles. The actual anomaly here is boring and uninteresting, and the story isn't solid enough to rescue that; I'm thinking, "Why make something so stupid?" rather than enjoying the tale.

I think the core idea, a guy deciding to give Bernie more money because he thinks that's what he needs, is decent, and I think the story isn't so weak as to be unsalvageable, but neither is done well enough. I did chuckle, and I think this could make a fine -J with a little work, so it's not all bad.

Still, downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder, if this was real, would upvoting the SCP page activate its effects?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a fair point, Wolf, but that does not talk the spotlight off of you. You simply said many others had said what you were going to say. The 'Discuss' section is for fresh feedback, but you literally added NOTHING.

item.title: Re: Adding recent political candidates is a mistake
item.content:encoded: I think that this is in here for the 'lols', brother. While the SCP Foundation is aware of these 'lols', I understand if you cannot comprehend them. They are an abstract concept, after all.

item.title: Re: There's a failure of redaction here.
item.content:encoded: I actually left it in intentionally as a small joke but it probably doesn't really make sense in-universe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry author, I love memes and all, and I get that's GAW's thing, but I just can't get behind this as an SCP. Well written, but I still feel like it was a 2433-j more like.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
GAW is really starting to develop in the community, even receiving the same type of complaints as AWCY, such as being in too silly and overstaying their welcome. Some may disagree, but this feels as if it's taking its place in the community.
Edit: If it causes controversy, it's probably going well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: headcanon: DonDeLillo is actually Don DeLillo irl

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's just stating that he would have said something, but most other people have already done that, he wants to let you know he don't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what exactly do your comments accomplish? I stated my reason for downvoting, as is my right to do, and to echo the criticisms made by others. If you have a problem with that, why don't you go harass every other person in the comments section of this skip who did the same thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As opposed to no information. What was said in this response that a simple downvote wouldn't say?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GreenWolf is telling us that they don't like the piece, and they don't like it for the reasons enumerated elsewhere in the thread. We have a marginally better knowledge of where the weight of opinion lies, which is useful. Writing their own long critique repeating everyone else's points would have given us exactly same amount of information.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Telling others why they're wrong for liking something you don't releases endorphins in the brain. So there's that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, in the end, what did this comment accomplish?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I could write a very long dissertation on why this is so terrible, but many other people have already done that.

Downvoted.

item.title: There's a failure of redaction here.
item.content:encoded:
kkrule: http://████████.tumblr.com/post/███████████/kkrulezzz-kkrulezzz-kkrulezzz-bernie-sanders-masterpost-funny

The tumblr URL is improperly redacted. If the Foundation wanted to redact kkrule's tumblr account, they should have redacted the three times it appears in the post URL. A properly redacted version would be: 
 kkrule: http://████████.tumblr.com/post/███████████/█████████-█████████-█████████-bernie-sanders-masterpost-funny

(Note: I have registered the tumblr URL kkrulezzz.tumblr.com in order to prevent it being unknowingly used by a real tumblr user in the future.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the concept of 'dank memes' is dumb and unsurprisingly, so is this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: title is much better now, but IDK I just don't enjoy this, this might be the first gamers againts weed that I don't like.

item.title: Re: Adding recent political candidates is a mistake
item.content:encoded:
That's why I said 'something' of an anomalous Anonymous.

I would imagine their hook is the extensive amount of reality tampering that they accomplish on a daily basis. That and I guess you can't rule out bluntfiend or one of the long-timers going out occasionally to recruit some new meat.

item.title: Re: You don't make a lot of Sense.
item.content:encoded:
Then why don't you ask me questions about what I mean? Because I can't tell what part you don't get of what I said.

So I'm going to start with the first thing. There is a difference between Get versus identify with. I was talking about people in the SCP Universe, not in this universe where writers and us readers live in. I thought I made it clear that I was talking about in their universe.

I actually popped over to the Japanese wikidot to see what you mean. They have the same exact kind of mild amusement concerning this skip as I did seeing what some short-sighted American decided to do. It had nothing to do with getting or identifying with GAW, because in a Foundation record entry, we're not meant to identify or get Gamers Against Weed. Didn't I make it clear that the skips aren't the problem, it's GAW itself? A real accurate comparison would be a translation of your tale, where we're supposed to understand the central character.

I actually do translation for a living. For Japan, the equivalent of the central character in your tale, is….Well, what do you think someone would be like when literally their ruling right-wing party has nearly continuously been in power since its founding close to 60 years ago? It's not disaffected youths that had been taking to the streets in Japan this past year, it was their teachers, their parents as well.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded:
I'm super trying to be nice, but you don't make a lot of sense. I think bluntfiend's story could feasibly happen in a different country. Is America the only country with disaffected youth? Like, GAW isn't even doing too bad on the translation sites, which shows that "non-Americans" apparently can understand the group.

Also the point of the name is its a joke. The point of it is they aren't taking it seriously. How does that make them Mary Sues? Are serious characters not allowed to tell jokes?

Also, keep in mind, I don't have to answer a single one of your questions. Your condescension and just constant demands don't really make me wanna engage meaningfully with you, buddy.

Like, have you really read them? Most of your problems are covered in Different Kinds of Nihilism, which you give a different name to every time you refer to it. Actually read things carefully. Most of your questions are answered.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded:
This is the mark to see precisely whether Gamers Against Weed will actually be able to find like-minded individuals to join them from other countries, aka expand and not be easy fodder to be gunned down like one of those reality-bender Mary-Sue Gary-Stu characters. I know what I think of that answer, but what is yours?

The Japanese translation of this article is at +9, which is a rarity since translated articles on that site, even the weird cool ones, typically do not get voted on, and if they do, it is not beyond +3 at the most.

Also:

I am asking you to think if this person can still have the same backstory in another country, growing up under a different government, different political news, different political campaigns etc. Would they still have decided to become what they are now?

You can not literally remove the entire premise or concept for every single group or every single plotline in literally any piece of media and have it translate across all languages and nations. Some pieces of work are contextualized within the boundaries of their premise (or, in this case, their politics). Which is irrelevant, because as I pointed out (see also, the Japanese site's tag for the group), people not from America seem to dig it.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded:
My presupposition of what? I am asking you to think if this person can still have the same backstory in another country, growing up under a different government, different political news, different political campaigns etc. Would they still have decided to become what they are now? This is the mark to see precisely whether Gamers Against Weed will actually be able to find like-minded individuals to join them from other countries, aka expand and not be easy fodder to be gunned down like one of those reality-bender Mary-Sue Gary-Stu characters. I know what I think of that answer, but what is yours?

Also, I had typed questions, plural. Meaning more than one question. You didn't answer the one I really wanted an answer from. Because the Site 19 chat really doesn't work for me.

I have a guess what the answer is, but at this point I think you have presuppositions of me. I just want for Gamers Against Weed to make sense. Right now, all the GAW SCPs are fine on their own if GAW didn't exist. They can be created by separate unaffiliated individuals and make sense. But Gamers Against Weed the group and the members themselves are the problem. If they, and their writers wanted to be taken seriously, than get rid of the name that is a joke. If it is to be a joke, than be ready to not be taken seriously as well. (I get the feeling that you were triggered when I said that only UIU is necessary to end them). Make it consistent about what the name means/comes from, about why they are to be taken seriously, answer why a non-American would want to be identified with them? Because if we model such a conversation between a founding member of Gamers Against Weed, and a curious prospective non-American, on our conversation just now, it'd go badly.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded: Well, uh, let me answer your question with another question. Does any work exist where if you take away the central character's backstory and worldview and, uh, the entire events of the story it'd still have the same message? I don't understand your presupposition. I don't get it.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded:
Huh? I didn't ask what their leader's name is, or said that their leader's name is anything else in the previous post.

It would also be really great if you can answer the questions I did ask in the previous post.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded: Their leader is named bluntfiend.

item.title: Re: Adding recent political candidates is a mistake
item.content:encoded:
I believe I was the one. That was me wondering, what is GAW? It's clear that they're a bunch of people on the internet with magic powers. So are they Anonymous? But then it was proven false, because Anonymous only started because it was a means to hide their tag, and the hackers that used the name Anonymous most likely never ever met each other or talked to each other. They're not a group or an organization, more like the GOI Nobody.

Given that GAW members talk to each other, it's more like an anomalous Gamergate. But then again, nothing so far shows that they care about gamers either, so they really have no hooks to draw in new members.

item.title: Re: could do with a dose of reality, or, barring that, GOC-issued bullets.
item.content:encoded:
Ah, because what I'm saying is everything your writing did not address. Since you are the writer, if A Different Kind of Nihilism have every single bit of reference to American Politics removed, would it still have the same message?

I was told by GAW fans that people join GAW by deciding to adopt the moniker online. So I am saying that given the fact that that they are "We are Gamers Against Weed," how is anyone supposed to understand that they are also a joke? Or did you absolutely forget that the internet is text and code only, non-verbal cues do not exist unlike face-to-face communication? Keep in mind that this very website has to have a disclaimer in the faq and in the rules to say "this is purely fictional and is just a community of writers" meaning do not take this seriously, and to keep away actual conspiracy theorists.

Because people outside of that guy's immediate chatroom buddy circle, they're only going to join GAW if they see GAW and what their members do, and like it. And since none of those GAW members make it clear in their chatlog that their name is absolutely a joke, or that specifically only non-violent people can join. They never put up a manifesto about what they're about. And they're never ever going to type constantly that they're a joke right after writing that they are Gamers Against Weed.

*sigh* Actually you guys, the writers that actually like GAW and formed them. I don't think you guys realized that there are more people living in countries where drug dealers are sentenced to death than there are people who live in countries that do not. Did you? And it's very easily taken as serious when the name "Gamers Against Weed" actually has been shown to people outside of your comfortable American left-leaning, Bernie-voting, circle of friends. Phillippines actually didn't have the death penalty and the vigilantes are doing what they do because they are tired of the rich ass dynasties that practically run their country like a feudal society. They're the ones downtrodden by their government system for decades, and why wouldn't they want to actually believe in the nice people with magic powers and join Gamers Against Weed? They were actually in the English news a bit so I actually dared to think that you Americans would at least notice that the rest of the world is different. Phillippines is actually one of the ones that actually knows better English than where I'm from.

That's why I say a good avenue to get Gamers Against Weed, to stop them from being nothing but self-indulgent Wish-Fulfillment. Is to start thinking of what they'd do when they come across actual people from other countries that have completely different sides of politics, and most importantly, when their name is not taken as a joke?
